mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already low and CPIAUCSL is elevated, a further inflation impulse raises the odds that markets price earlier policy tightening; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as inflation expectations and term premium reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL rises while the 10-year yield falls in most 20-day windows, or if the sign of the CPIAUCSL-yield relationship is negative in a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a near-term threshold signals weaker labor demand and slower growth; investors price a lower path for real rates and inflation, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE upticks are followed by higher 10-year yields in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day yield change after triggers is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger retail sales together with firmer employment indicate broad nominal demand; when RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, markets anticipate stronger growth and less imminent easing, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and PAYEMS up does not coincide with positive 20-day yield changes, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in INDPRO relative to HOUST reflects stronger real activity without an equivalent housing slowdown; this lifts growth expectations and term premium, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if a rising INDPRO-to-HOUST spread is not associated with higher 60-day yields, or if the relationship flips sign in manufacturing expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL stops decelerating, markets infer that policy must stay restrictive for longer; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as the expected path of short rates shifts upward.
failure_condition: Reject if, under FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, non-decelerating CPIAUCSL is followed by flat or lower 10-year yields in most cases, or if the 20-day average yield change is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 following prior strength signals decelerating final demand; investors revise down real-growth expectations and lower the expected path of yields, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth slows versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns are followed by higher 60-day yields more often than lower yields, or if the sign of the average 60-day yield response is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor market, a combination of rising UNRATE and softer PAYEMS signals weakening income growth and reduced inflation pressure; the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days because markets price easier policy and lower real rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous UNRATE increases and PAYEMS declines do not precede lower 20-day yields, or if the interaction adds no predictive power over each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When housing activity weakens and retail sales also soften, the demand mix points to broad cyclical deceleration; the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 5 trading days as traders front-run weaker growth data.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline versus their prior month
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS are not followed by lower 5-day yields, or if the average response is zero or positive in a rolling out-of-sample test.
